Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Imposing Evidence, Issue, And Monetary Sanctions Against Deft'S And/Or Their Counsel
Off Calendar Jun-11-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE
Brendan MacCauley, Esq., appeared for Plaintiff and moving party: Transitional Investors
Bennett Young, , Esq., appeared for Defendant and opposing party: Michael Jackson

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Transitional Investors LLC.'s Motion For An Order Imposing Evidence, Issue, And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants and/or Their Counsel is denied. The notice of motion seeks sanctions and not further production. Further produce must be sought before the court will determine that there has been a failure to production that warrants the requested sanctions. The order that is the basis of this motion did not determine whether objections were properly made. The order provided that discovery should proceed upon the entry of a protective order; and importantly, further determined that objections had not been waived. If Plaintiff wished to have the court review the objections which had been made prior to the hearing, that review should have been requested.
By its terms, the order did not require the production of documents for which Defendant claimed privilege. This motion incorrectly assumes that the court has made a prior determination regarding any assertion of privilege or the scope of production that is required. Without that determination having been made, Plaintiff's assertion that there have been improper objections and inadequate production pursuant to those objections has no merit.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
